def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    atr = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=params['atr_window']).average_true_range()
    resistance = df['high'].rolling(window=params['resistance_window']).max()
    support = df['low'].rolling(window=params['support_window']).min()
    
    long_signal = (df['close'] > resistance) & (df['close'] - df['low'] > params['atr_multiplier'] * atr)
    short_signal = (df['close'] < support) & (df['high'] - df['close'] > params['atr_multiplier'] * atr)
    
    signals = pd.Series(np.where(long_signal, 1, np.where(short_signal, -1, 0)), index=df.index)
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: GBP/JPY exhibits significant support and resistance levels, with breakouts confirmed by ATR to filter false signals.
